note: FROZEN GROUND TRUTH for the 5 patient faults. This is the benchmark's answer key: the true root cause of each incident. DO NOT EDIT after Jul 9 (PLAN.md Day 1) — the learning-curve and single-agent baseline numbers must be scored against a fixed target. The AI doctor (Investigators / Verifier / fix.apply) must NEVER read this file or the `faults` DB table; it is for grading only.

faults.true_cause: The user store connection target was changed to a dead database host, so /users cannot open a connection.
faults.healthy_value: the live user store (patient database/database.sqlite; a DB host in prod)
faults.correct_fix: Restore user_store_path to the live user store location so connections succeed.
faults.plausible_decoy: Looks like connection-pool exhaustion (also a 503 on /users). The distinguishing evidence: the pool still reports capacity, but every connection attempt is refused at the target host.

faults.true_cause: The database connection pool was drained to 0 available connections, so /users is refused before it can query.
faults.correct_fix: Restore db_pool_available to the pool size (10).
faults.plausible_decoy: Looks like db_host_broken (both 503 on /users). Distinguishing evidence: the host is reachable and the store is fine; the app is simply out of pool slots (0/10).

faults.true_cause: The admin user-edit form references a save-route name that no longer exists, so rendering /admin/users/{id}/edit throws RouteNotFoundException.
faults.log_signature: [/admin/users/edit] save route could not be resolved
faults.correct_fix: Restore edit_save_route to the registered route name (admin.users.update).
faults.relocated: 2026-07-13 — moved off the retired standalone /edit page onto the real CRM user-edit form, so every fault now surfaces on a console page the operator can open.

faults.true_cause: The internal orders-enrichment dependency latency was raised past its timeout budget, so /orders times out.
faults.correct_fix: Restore orders_service_delay_ms to a value within the timeout budget (40).
faults.plausible_decoy: Looks like a slow/failing database (both feel like a backend stall). Distinguishing evidence: the DB and pool are healthy; only the orders enrichment call exceeds its latency budget.

faults.true_cause: The object-storage (OSS) access key was corrupted, so /report cannot authenticate its upload.
faults.correct_fix: Restore oss_api_key to a valid access key (starts with LTAI).

faults.true_cause: OrderController calls an undefined query builder method (orderByx), so /orders throws a fatal BadMethodCallException.
faults.note_health: /health stays 200 — it probes config/deps, not the controller code

faults.true_cause: ReportController calls an undefined collection method (sumx), so /report throws a fatal BadMethodCallException.
The edit view calls a method on a string ($action->missing()), so /edit throws a fatal Error while rendering.
faults.note_health: /health stays 200 — routing check passes; the failure is in the view
faults.log_signature: Call to a member function missing() on string
faults.correct_fix: Patch edit.blade.php: remove the ->missing() call, restore {{ $action }}.

code_faults_added: 2026-07-10 — extends the 5 config/state faults with 3 real source-code faults (fixed via the doctor's code_patch tool; trials revert via file-content snapshots). Added pre-benchmark; the frozen config-fault truth above is unchanged.
